findings.title: Non-verifier leases can be upgraded into parent authority without begin capability
findings.rationale: The documented contract says begin-loop-lease requires CODEX1_PARENT_LOOP_BEGIN=1 and reviewer lanes must not receive that capability, but the implementation only enforces that when no lease exists. If load_ralph_loop_lease returns Some(existing) with no parent_authority_verifier, the Some(_) arm falls through and the caller receives a fresh verifier-backed parent_authority_token. That leaves legacy or stale non-verifier loop leases as a path for a child/reviewer lane to mint parent loop authority, after which the lane can satisfy the token guard used for review truth capture and outcome writeback. The regression covers only the no-lease case, so this upgrade path is not proven closed.
findings.suggested_next_action: Require CODEX1_PARENT_LOOP_BEGIN=1 whenever begin-loop-lease creates or replaces verifier-backed authority from a non-verifier lease, or reject legacy non-verifier leases until the parent explicitly clears/upgrades them; add a regression with an existing loop-lease.json lacking parent_authority_verifier.
